description: Define the core data model in khipu/model.py — Session, Exchange, ToolCall, Outcome dataclasses with to_dict/from_dict serialization. This is the normalized format all ingestors produce and all analyzers consume.
title: Trace model: Session, Exchange, ToolCall, Outcome dataclasses

description: Implement the emit stage. emit(result, template='markdown') renders an AnalysisResult through a Jinja2 template. Default markdown template produces a human-readable report with sections for workflows, patterns, and crystallization scores. --emit flag on the CLI.
title: Emitter: Jinja2 markdown output template

description: Implement deterministic condensation (no LLM). Strip tool call I/O bodies, keep tool names/file paths/success booleans. First 100 chars of human messages, 80 chars of agent messages. Output structured JSON summary per session. Auto mode: estimate tokens as len/4, condense if sessions exceed 70% of backend context_limit. Flags: --condense (always), --no-condense (never).
title: Condensation: deterministic session compression

description:
Implement the analysis engine: load prompt files with YAML frontmatter, resolve depends_on DAG, inject {sessions}/{patterns}/{workflows} template variables, call LLM backend, extract JSON with retry logic.

Define AnalysisResult and ResultMetadata dataclasses (output types of this stage):
- AnalysisResult: timestamp, session_count, sessions_skipped, workflows, patterns, crystallization, custom, metadata
- ResultMetadata: backend, model, condensation_mode, prompt_versions (dict analyzer_id->version), duration_ms

CLI backend mode: shell out via TOML config. Built-in backend: claude_cli.toml. Command format: pipe prompt via stdin ('echo "$prompt" | claude -p') rather than shell argument to avoid quoting/length issues. context_limit=180000.

Built-in prompts: workflows.md, patterns.md, crystallize.md (all three from the spec). JSON extraction with one retry on parse failure.
title: Analysis engine: DAG runner, prompt loader, Claude CLI backend

description: Implement the ingestor discovery engine with two-phase detection (extension → content sniff) and priority resolution. Built-in ingestors: claude_code.py (JSONL with tool_use/tool_result), generic.py (markdown fallback, PRIORITY=-100). Engine handles directory recursion; ingestors parse single files.
title: Ingestor engine + claude_code and generic built-in ingestors

description: Implement automatic redaction that runs before any content reaches an LLM. Default patterns: API keys (sk-, ghp_, Bearer, AKIA), connection strings (postgres://, mongodb://, redis://), private keys, env var assignments, AWS secrets, email addresses, IP addresses. Replace with [REDACTED:type] tokens. --no-redact flag with stderr warning.
title: Redaction: strip secrets before LLM sees anything

description: Set up pyproject.toml with uv/hatch, khipu package structure, and typer CLI entry point. khipu analyze <path> wired to a no-op stub. khipu --version works. This is the foundation everything else builds on.
title: Project scaffold: khipu package and CLI entry point

description: Connect ingest → redact → condense → analyze → emit into the khipu analyze command. Progress output to stderr, result to stdout. Stdin support: khipu analyze - --ingestor claude_code. Acceptance: khipu analyze ./traces/ produces a markdown report with workflows, patterns, and crystallization scores from real Claude Code session files.
title: Wire end-to-end: khipu analyze runs all 5 stages
